articles.title: Trump administration is allegedly collecting $10 billion on the TikTok deal
articles.summary: In September, Donald Trump claimed that "the United States is getting a tremendous fee " for brokering the TikTok deal. Now sources tell the Wall Street Journal and the New York Times that fee is expected to be in the range of $10 billion. The money is supposedly being paid by ne

articles.title: Microsoft releases Windows 11 OOB hotpatch to fix RRAS RCE flaw
articles.summary: Microsoft has released an out-of-band (OOB) update to fix a security vulnerabilities affecting Windows 11 Enterprise devices that receive hotpatch updates instead of the regular Patch Tuesday cumulative updates. [...]

articles.title: The MacBook Neo is ‘the most repairable MacBook’ in years, according to iFixit
articles.summary: Apple’s new MacBook Neo isn’t just the most affordable MacBook — it’s also the company's most repairable laptop in “about fourteen years."

articles.title: US Army announces contract with Anduril worth up to $20B
articles.summary: The Army described this as a single enterprise contract consolidating more than 120 separate "procurement actions."

articles.title: Meta is reportedly laying off up to 20 percent of its staff

articles.title: Meta reportedly considering layoffs that could affect 20% of the company
articles.summary: These layoffs could help Facebook's parent company offset its aggressive spending on AI infrastructure, as well as AI-related acquisitions and hiring.

articles.title: Japan Approves the World’s First Treatment Made With Reprogrammed Human Cells
articles.summary: Researchers in Japan pioneered reprogrammed cells 20 years ago. Now the country has given the first-ever authorizations to manufacture and sell medical products based on the technology.
